page.content: The data presented in the accompanying table provides a comprehensive overview of the factors contributing to delays in patient treatment within community mental health clinics utilizing Internet-based Cognitive Behavioral Therapy (ICBT). Specifically, the "Reason for Delay" column highlights various challenges faced by clinics, while the "Number of Patients (%)" column quantifies the extent to which these delays affect service delivery.

lines.text: The data presented in the accompanying table provides a comprehensive overview of the factors contributing to delays in
patient treatment within community mental health clinics utilizing Internet-based Cognitive Behavioral Therapy (ICBT).
Specifically, the "Reason for Delay" column highlights various challenges faced by clinics, while the "Number of Patients (%)"
column quantifies the extent to which these delays affect service delivery.

page.content: Additionally, the regional context is illustrated through the "Region of Community Mental Health Clinic," which identifies the geographical distribution of clinics participating in the survey. In the case of Regina Qu’Appelle, with an adult population of 218,783, the statistics indicate that a significant proportion of patients are experiencing treatment delays linked to the choice of surgeon, exemplifying a critical area for improvement. At the time of the survey, clinics had varying levels of experience with ICBT, as denoted by the "# of months clinic used ICBT at time of survey." This information is vital for understanding how operational factors, such as the number of therapists trained in ICBT—totaling 40 across participating clinics—affect patient outcomes and service efficiency. The insights gleaned from this data can inform strategies aimed at enhancing clinic operations and ultimately improving patient care.
